title: two-level WolfHsmError (Wh vs Ffi) should not be collapsed
description: WolfHsmError distinguishes Wh { code } (wolfHSM protocol errors in range -2302..-2000) from Ffi { code, func } (wolfSSL/wolfCrypt errors outside that range). Why this matters: callers that need to handle 'key not found' or 'NVM full' programmatically can match on WolfHsmError::Wh and inspect the code without doing arithmetic on raw i32 values. If both were collapsed to a single Ffi variant, callers would have to check numeric ranges to distinguish HSM-layer errors from transport-layer errors. The DataLost and AlreadyRegistered variants similarly carry typed semantic meaning that cannot be recovered from a raw code. A future contributor might suggest 'simplify to one error variant' to reduce the enum size — this would degrade the caller experience for any code that needs to handle specific error conditions programmatically.
